item.title: First Impressions from Sabhal Mòr Ostaig
item.author: Emery Jacobowitz
item.description:
First Impressions from Sabhal Mòr Ostaig

This is the first of a series of reflections I wrote during my time at Sabhal Mòr Ostaig, an accreditted college on the Sleat peninsula of the Isle of Skye. SMO is unique in that it offers Gaelic-language higher education in a number of fields, and is home to many of the leading academics in the field of Gaelic revitalization.

In this reflection, I respond to the paper Not enough words: Language acquisition and identity work in tertiary-level Gaelic-medium education. by Dr. Tim Currie Armstrong, whose lectures I attended on several occasions.

More…

item.title: Hello World
item.author: Emery Jacobowitz
item.description:
This is the introduction to my blog. I plan to use this site to publish projects I’ve worked on, as well as personal thoughts on various topics. Most of what I post will pertain to at least one of programming, Language and linguistics, or whatever has captured my interest at the moment.

My first few posts are going to be reflections from my Summer spent learning about linguistic fieldwork in small communities of Scotland.

- Emery
